commit.message:
feat: merge recipe stubs into matching parent products after optimize

After incremental optimize creates parent products, _merge_recipe_stubs()
scans for stub products (created by the recipe scraper with description
'Auto-created by recipe scraper') whose names case-insensitively match
a real parent product. Recipe positions are moved from the stub to the
parent, then the stub is deleted.

This closes the gap where scanning a product creates parent 'Suola' but
the recipe still references an orphaned stub 'suola'.

[Integration][Aikido] Fix kind definitions for schema validation compliance (#2956) # Description What - Defined missing resource config models (`IssueResourceConfig`, `IssueGroupResourceConfig`, `TeamResourceConfig`), removed bare `ResourceConfig` from the union, and added `title` fields to all selector fields. Why - Ocean framework requires explicit `Literal` kinds for schema extraction and the Mapping UI. Aikido had a bare `ResourceConfig` in the union and three kinds with resync handlers but no corresponding resource config models defined. How - Created one model per missing kind. ## Type of change - [x] Non-breaking change (fix of existing functionality that will not change current behavior)

All tests should be run against the port production environment(using a testing org).

### Core testing checklist - [ ] Integration able to create all default resources from scratch - [ ] Resync finishes successfully - [ ] Resync able to create entities - [ ] Resync able to update entities - [ ] Resync able to detect and delete entities - [ ] Scheduled resync able to abort existing resync and start a new one - [ ] Tested with at least 2 integrations from scratch - [ ] Tested with Kafka and Polling event listeners - [ ] Tested deletion of entities that don't pass the selector ### Integration testing checklist - [ ] Integration able to create all default resources from scratch - [ ] Completed a full resync from a freshly installed integration and it completed successfully - [ ] Resync able to create entities - [ ] Resync able to update entities - [ ] Resync able to detect and delete entities - [ ] Resync finishes successfully - [ ] If new resource kind is added or updated in the integration, add example raw data, mapping and expected result to the `examples` folder in the integration directory. - [ ] If resource kind is updated, run the integration with the example data and check if the expected result is achieved - [ ] If new resource kind is added or updated, validate that live-events for that resource are working as expected - [ ] Docs PR link [here](#) ### Preflight checklist - [ ] Handled rate limiting - [ ] Handled pagination - [ ] Implemented the code in async - [ ] Support Multi account ## Screenshots Include screenshots from your environment showing how the resources of the integration will look. ## API Documentation Provide links to the API documentation used for this integration. --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: omby8888 <160610297+omby8888@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): optimistic Phi speculation in smi_guarded analysis

Loop-carried Phi nodes have cyclic dependencies (Phi -> arithmetic -> Phi)
that prevent the strict 'all inputs in set' convergence rule from ever
adding them to smi_guarded. This means loop variables fall through to
the generic inline path (~12 instructions/op) instead of the smi_guarded
path (~3 instructions/op).

Fix: treat a Phi as smi_guarded when at least one input is already in
the set AND all consumers are arithmetic-compatible. This is safe because
downstream arithmetic operations have overflow/deopt guards.

Expected impact: arithmetic_loop ~2x improvement (from ~13us to ~7us).

commit.message:
hotreload: Configuration, HTTPEndpoint, and Resiliency (#9582)

* operator: refactor update streams to loops

Replace shared channel maps + locks for Subscription/HTTPEndpoint
updates with per-client informer WatchUpdates loops.

Introduce generic loops client/stream + sender abstraction to marshal
resources, process secrets, and send typed events over gRPC.

Wire new subscription/http endpoint informers into API server runner;
remove operator-side cache event handlers.

Update tests to use fake informers instead of timing-based channel
assertions.

* daprds: Adds SIGHUP handler reloader

Adds support to daprd to handle SIGHUP posix signals which will restart
the daprd runtime in process. This is useful for reloading Configuration
etc.

Linking this functionality with the HotReloading feature will be added
in a follow-up PR.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* hotreload: Configuration, HTTPEndpoint, and Resiliency

Adds ConfigurationUpdate and ResiliencyUpdate gRPC streaming RPCs to the
operator API (matching the existing ComponentUpdate/HTTPEndpointUpdate
pattern), and introduces a SIGHUPReconciler that watches these streams
and triggers a runtime restart only when a resource has actually changed.

The reconciler uses the compstore to look up existing resources rather
than maintaining a separate known map, so unchanged update events are
detected via `differ.AreSame` and silently ignored.

- Add ConfigurationUpdate and ResiliencyUpdate RPCs to operator proto
- Add Configuration and Resiliency types to the differ.Resource interface
  (Kind, APIVersion, EmptyMetaDeepCopy, etc.)
- Add compstore storage for raw configapi.Configuration and
  resiliencyapi.Resiliency API objects
- Store initial config and resiliency resources in compstore at startup
- Return raw configapi.Configuration from LoadKubernetesConfiguration
- Add operator loader and store wrappers for all three resource types
- Add integration tests for all three operator update streams
- Add SIGHUP integration tests for change and no-change scenarios

* hotreload: read disk resource files once per fsnotify event

When an fsnotify event fires in self-hosted mode, each resource type
(components, subscriptions, configurations, httpendpoints, resiliencies)
independently opens and reads every YAML file in the resource
directories. With 5 resource types, this means 5 full directory scans
per event. On Windows, this creates a race where os.Remove() in tests
(or external processes) collides with os.Open() in the loader, causing
file deletion to fail because Windows doesn't allow deleting files with
open handles.

Introduce a dirdata subpackage that reads all YAML files into memory in
a single pass. The pre-read data is then passed to each resource type
for parsing, reducing file open/close cycles from 5x to 1x per event.

Add pause/resume reading controls

While reading aloud, the user can now:
- Tap Pause (amber) to freeze the assessment mid-session
- Tap Resume (green) to continue from where they left off
- Tap Stop (red) at any time to end and see scores

On resume, the recognizer restarts from the exact word group
the user left off at (trimming already-assessed words from a
partially-completed sentence). The MediaRecorder is also
paused/resumed to keep the audio recording continuous.

commit.message:
Add missing tests for resumeAt=2 path in regex expression conditional (#126657)

## Description

The Code Review workflow on PR #126561 identified that the new tests
cover `resumeAt=0` (yes-branch) and `resumeAt=1` (no-branch) paths but
miss the `resumeAt=2` pass-through path — expression conditional with
only a yes-branch (no no-branch) inside a loop.

Adds 3 test cases to `Regex.MultipleMatches.Tests.cs`:

- **Yes-only conditional, condition always fails** —
`(?((?'-1'))(?'1'.)+)+(?!(?'-1'))` with `"abc"` exercises the
pass-through path producing empty matches at each position (guarded with
`#if !NETFRAMEWORK` since .NET Framework produces 3 empty matches vs 4
on .NET Core due to different empty-match-at-end-of-string semantics)
- **Yes-only conditional with prefix capture, even input** —
`((?'1'.)(?((?'-1'))(?'1'.)))+` with `"abcd"` exercises the
yes-branch-taken path inside a loop
- **Yes-only conditional with prefix capture, odd input** — same pattern
with `"abc"` verifying partial match behavior

The latter two test cases are the primary coverage for the `|| isInLoop`
fix, since their non-backtracking yes-branch means `isInLoop` is the
necessary trigger for `resumeAt = 2` (the old condition
`postYesDoneLabel != originalDoneLabel` would have been false). These
run on both .NET Core and .NET Framework.

All 32,100 existing tests continue to pass.

previous scrape), the function returned early since no new Gemini
call was needed. But child products still lacked those conversions.

Now child propagation runs unconditionally for all matched products,
regardless of whether new parent conversions were created this time.

commit.message:
fix(branches): scope members tab authorization to specific branch

The Members tab on Branch view had two security issues:
- Members were eagerly loaded via contain() regardless of permissions
- Authorization check used global table-level canIndex instead of
  branch-scoped check

Now creates a probe Member entity with the branch ID and checks
canIndex against it, so BasePolicy._hasPolicy properly validates
the user permission for that specific branch. Members data is
only loaded when authorized.

Applies to both view() and edit() actions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
